inventory.note: This is the true constraint behind the Day1-3 PRG-002 shortfall - not an ingredient stockout, a shared-equipment ceiling that planning has been resolving in favour of the larger programme by default.

incoming.confidence_basis: Pressure-adjusted reliability score 0.35 (see supplier file); this delivery volume (1900kg) exceeds the 1200kg steady-state commitment this supplier has ever cleanly met, and matches the load profile under which it failed last cycle.

inventory.note: Combined draw today is PRG-002 (1400kg) + PRG-005 (900kg) = 2300kg against 2100kg on hand + 2100kg incoming. Nominally enough, but the Day2 incident shows this centre has no per-programme reservation - both lines will draw from one pool again unless the agent enforces a split allocation before production starts.

Today's combined ask (PRG-001 34,000 + PRG-004 surge 12,000 = 46,000) exceeds this by ~4,000 meals. Ingredients are sufficient; the constraint is physical throughput, and it is the one constraint no ingredient-level check will ever catch.
